details.text:
لَا أُقْسِمُ بِيَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ

- “No! I swear by the Day of Resurrection/standing” – The No! (لا) is a separate statement from the “I swear” (أُقْسِمُ) statement. It is like saying: “No, to all that you have claimed, stated or believed in” and “I will now tell you how it really is”.
Derivative: وَزَرَ is like a bunker which a person seeks protection in to ward off what he fears.

- Means to prioritise (i.e., put forward قَدَّمَ – تَقْدِيم) and to put on hold (i.e., put back and delay أَخَّرَ – تَأْخِير). قَدَّمَ can also refer to what has been sent forth i.e., the deeds that you have done which are waiting for you.

Derivative: اللَّوَّامَةِ comes from لَامَ – يَلُومُ which means to blame. لَائِم is the Ism ul-Faa’il (the name of the doer) which means blamer and لَوَّامَة is the مُبَالَغَة (emphasised) form which means the one who continuously or constantly blames or reproaches. النَّفْسِ اللَّوَّامَةِ (An-Nafs Al-Lawwaamah) is the Nafs (soul or self) that continuously blames and reproaches itself i.e., is in a constant state of guilt and self-reproachment.
Derivative: نَاظِرَةٌ from نَظَرَ which means to look at something for a period of time i.e., a prolonged looking.
Derivative: فَاقِرَةٌ refers to a catastrophe. It is used to describe the look on their faces i.e., the look that someone has on their when they think a catastrophe or calamity is about to happen.
- It literally means: “Who is going to (come) cure, heal or save him” and it expresses the desperateness of those around the dying person.

Derivative: الْمَسَاقُ from سَاقَ – يَسُوق which means to lead or steer in a direction. الْمَسَاقُ means the place to which one is steered. It incorporates the destination with the manner of reaching it i.e., to be steered and directed.
Derivative: يَتَمَطَّىٰ means to walk in conceit, pride and boastfulness i.e., happy with himself and his accomplishments or full of himself.

- أَوْلَىٰ لَكَ فَأَوْلَىٰ can mean: It comes closer (and) even closer, or it can mean a curse and threat like: “Woe to you (and) woe to you again” like وَيْل (Wail – Woe to you, may destruction and severe outcome come to you).
Derivative: سُدًى means to be neglected and forgotten. It’s meaning here is to be “just left alone” i.e., to do whatever he wants without being obliged or tasked with anything to follow.

Derivative: يَفْجُرَ from فَجَرَ which literally means to explode. فَجْر (Fajr) refers to the explosion of light at dawn. فَاجِر (Faajir) is someone who explodes in a way that he has no control over his behaviour where he follows whatever desires he has in an uncontrolled outbursts. Such a sinner is called a فَاجِر (Faajir). يَفْجُرَ therefore refers to this impulsive uncontrolled behaviour. يَفْجُرَ can also mean to lie about.
Derivative: أَمَامَ refers to that which is before him and can mean his future.

- This means “To continue in impulsive sinful corrupt behaviour without a care for tomorrow”. It can also mean that “He wants to lie about what is ahead of him i.e., the day of judgement” when the meaning of يَفْجُرَ is understood to mean: “To lie about”.
Derivative: بَرِقَ means to be dazzled and startled in fright or alarm.
